document_name: Codex implementation instructions: free public snow-depth refresh workflow
audience: Codex running in GitHub Codespaces or another development environment
objective: Implement a Rails background refresh system that collects snow base/depth data for every resort using free public web pages only, including valid 0-inch observations for closed, pre-season, off-season, summer-operations, or no-snow resorts when supported by source text.

explicit_user_direction.public_page_workflow_permission: The project owner has stated they have permission to view public pages in this workflow. Do not block implementation on a generic public-page scraping concern. Build a polite, cache-backed, low-frequency fetch workflow.
explicit_user_direction.openai_role: Allowed as a parser/extractor over source text that the Rails app fetches and caches. The app should not rely on OpenAI browsing independently.

source_priority.category: Known official structured endpoints or feeds already discovered
examples: Palisades Tahoe / Alpine Meadows mtnpowder feeds
Whistler Blackcomb official PDF/print report if parsable
source_priority.notes: Use deterministic provider-specific parsers where source structure is known.

source_priority.category: Official resort snow, mountain, weather, current conditions, PDF, or operations pages
source_priority.notes: Fetch page or PDF, extract machine text, then use deterministic parsing first and OpenAI structured extraction as fallback.

examples: OpenSnow country or region snow-report listing pages
source_priority.notes: Good broad fallback for base depth and closed/0-inch states. Distinguish resort-reported values from modeled/estimated values when the page indicates that distinction.
Use as free public fallback when official source and OpenSnow are unavailable or unparseable. Preserve source_name and source_url.

examples: SnoCountry public pages if usable without a paid feed
source_priority.notes: Use cautiously, record staleness and provenance, and prefer official/OpenSnow/OnTheSnow when available.

zero_inches_is_valid_when: The source explicitly reports base depth as 0 inches or 0 cm.
The source explicitly states no snow.
The source explicitly states closed, pre-season, off-season, summer operations, or season concluded and does not publish a contradictory nonzero current snow-depth value.
The source shows closed/0 trails/0 lifts plus no current ski-season base-depth number, and surrounding source context supports closed or off-season status.
do_not_store_zero_when: The page is merely blank, blocked, inaccessible, or JavaScript-only without visible closed/no-snow evidence.
The value is missing but the operating status is unknown.
The only available value is stale and does not describe current conditions.
The source publishes an explicit nonzero current base depth even though the resort is closed. Preserve the explicit nonzero value and mark operating status closed.
data_policy.null_means: Unknown, unavailable, unparseable, stale, blocked, or unsupported by source evidence.
data_policy.unit_conversion: Normalize stored snow-depth values to inches. Convert centimeters to inches using cm / 2.54. Decimal inches are acceptable when preserving conversion fidelity; otherwise round consistently in the UI layer.

recommended_rails_models.name: SnowReportSource
recommended_rails_models.purpose: Configured public source URL(s) for each resort and provider.

suggested_fields.provider_name: string, e.g. official_resort, opensnow, onthesnow, skiresort_info
suggested_fields.parser_strategy: string, e.g. deterministic, openai_text_extraction, pdf_text_extraction, html_text_extraction

recommended_rails_models.name: SnowSourceFetch
recommended_rails_models.purpose: Audit/cache record for each page fetch before extraction.

recommended_rails_models.name: SnowObservation
recommended_rails_models.purpose: Normalized snow-depth observation shown by the app.

suggested_fields.operating_status: string: open, closed, preseason, offseason, summer_operations, unknown
suggested_fields.zero_depth_reason: nullable string: explicit_zero, closed, preseason, offseason, summer_operations, no_snow

recommended_rails_models.name: SnowRefreshRun
recommended_rails_models.purpose: Batch-level refresh metadata for daily/weekly jobs.

recommended_service_objects.responsibility: Scheduled ActiveJob that enqueues or performs refreshes for all active resorts/sources at daily or weekly cadence.
Refresh one resort by trying active SnowReportSource records in priority order until a valid observation is produced, while recording failed attempts.
HTTP fetch public source pages with timeouts, a clear user agent, gzip support, redirects, and polite low-frequency behavior. Cache raw response text and metadata.
Convert HTML to compact relevant text before parsing or OpenAI extraction. Remove nav, footer, scripts, styles, and obvious unrelated text.
Extract text from official PDF/print reports when needed.
Provider-specific parsers for known sources such as Hakuba Valley, mtnpowder feeds, Aspen WeatherDashboard, and other stable structures.
Call the OpenAI API with a strict JSON schema to extract snow report fields from fetched source text. Must not browse independently.
Validate units, timestamps, source evidence, zero-depth policy, numeric ranges, and confidence before saving SnowObservation.

openai_extraction_contract.model_selection: Use a current cost-effective OpenAI model that supports structured outputs. Read OPENAI_API_KEY from Rails credentials or environment variables. Never commit secrets.
openai_extraction_contract.input: Trimmed source text or PDF text fetched by Rails, plus resort name, source URL, source name, source type, and current refresh timestamp.
rules_for_prompt: Return JSON only matching the schema.
Do not browse or rely on outside knowledge.
Use null for missing numeric values.
base_depth_inches may be 0 only if source text supports explicit zero, no snow, closed, pre-season, off-season, or summer operations.
If source text reports an explicit nonzero snow depth for a closed resort, preserve the nonzero snow depth and mark operating_status closed.
Include short source_evidence that justifies the extracted depth/status.
If the source is stale or only describes a past season, set confidence low or return no valid observation depending on validator policy.

observed_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp if present in source text

implementation_steps: Read AGENTS.md and existing research CSV/docs before coding.
Create database migrations for SnowReportSource, SnowSourceFetch, SnowObservation, and SnowRefreshRun or align names with existing app conventions if similar models already exist.
Seed SnowReportSource from data/snow_report_sources.csv and add additional OpenSnow/OnTheSnow/Skiresort.info URLs as discovered.
Build deterministic parsers for the known structured sources first, using data/snow_endpoint_candidates.csv as the starting map.
Build SourceFetcher with timeouts, redirects, response-size limits, caching, retry backoff, and a polite user agent identifying the app.
Build HtmlTextExtractor and optional PdfTextExtractor so OpenAI receives compact relevant text, not entire noisy pages.
Build OpenAiExtractor using structured outputs and the schema in this JSON file.
Build ObservationValidator enforcing the zero-depth policy and rejecting unsupported inferred zeros.
Implement RefreshResortJob to try sources by priority and save the best valid observation while keeping fetch/audit records.
Add a daily or weekly schedule using the project's chosen Rails scheduling mechanism, e.g. Solid Queue recurring tasks if configured.
Add admin/developer rake tasks for one-off refreshes: all resorts, one resort by id/name, and one provider/source URL.
Expose latest SnowObservation in resort views only after validation; display Unknown/Unavailable for null rather than converting null to 0.
Write model, service, parser, validator, and job specs. Include fixture pages for closed/0, explicit nonzero closed, missing value, cm conversion, stale source, and blocked/unparseable source cases.
acceptance_criteria: Every Resort can have one or more configured SnowReportSource records.
A refresh run can attempt all resorts without requiring paid APIs or licensed feeds.
OpenAI API calls happen only after Rails fetches source text; OpenAI is not asked to browse independently.
Closed/no-snow resorts may produce 0-inch base observations only with source evidence.
Each SnowObservation stores source URL, source name, queried_at, observed_at when available, extraction method, confidence, and evidence/notes.
Known explicit nonzero depths for closed resorts are preserved and marked closed instead of overwritten to 0.
The workflow can run daily or weekly in a background job and is safe to retry.
No API keys or secrets are committed. OPENAI_API_KEY is read from Rails credentials or environment variables.

job_specs: RefreshResortJob tries sources in priority order
RefreshResortJob records failed fetches without crashing whole run

important_non_goals: Do not add paid API integrations.
Do not commit OpenAI API keys or provider credentials.
Do not hard-code Ikon/Epic logic into Resort.
Do not show null snow depth as 0 in the UI.
Do not make live web requests from page-rendering controller actions; use background jobs and cached latest observations.
socratic_questions_for_implementer: Which source should be considered authoritative when official and aggregator values disagree but both are fresh?
How stale can an observation be before the UI should label it stale or unavailable?
Should residual snowpack on a closed resort be useful to users, or should UI emphasize operating status first?
Should each base/mid/summit depth be tied to a ResortLocation row when the source provides location labels?
